Monash College · UCB

Who I am

A kid building
his story.

I'm Swarup Raiyan Swasti — an IT student, writer, rapper, and idea-chaser studying a Diploma of Information Technology at Monash College, delivered through Universal College Bangladesh. I grew up between Dhaka and West Bengal, India, and every city shaped how I see the world.

My strongest skills aren't only technical — they're communicative. I've written original rap songs, performed on stage, led Model UN events, anchored school ceremonies, debated in competitions, and built websites. I believe the best technologists aren't just coders — they're people who can think, create, persuade, and lead.

"I don't just want to work in tech. I want to build things that change what's possible."

What drives me

Personal Interests

🎤 Words, Rhythm & Stage
I write original rap, poems, speeches, and stories. Performing them on stage, in competitions, in front of a crowd — that's where I feel most alive. Writing and performance are how I think out loud.
🧠 Ideas & Debate
Philosophy, politics, history, human behaviour. I love exploring why people think the way they do, and arguing for and against ideas until I understand them from every angle. MUN shaped this instinct.
🌐 Technology & Building
I'm genuinely excited by what code can create. I tinker with Python, experiment with web design, and obsess over how digital tools can be used creatively. I want to build things that didn't exist before.
Rap & Songwriting Poetry Debate Model UN Public Speaking Guitar Web Design Python Philosophy Storytelling

Where I'm headed

Career Ambitions

Build things that
didn't exist yesterday.

My ambition isn't a job title — it's a way of operating. I want to combine my technology education with my creative instincts and communication skills to build products, companies, or creative works that actually matter. I'm drawn to entrepreneurship not for the status, but because I genuinely believe in making things from scratch. Technology is my lever. Creativity is my edge. Communication is how I bring people along.

Technology & ProductProgress from the Diploma of IT into a CS or IT degree at Monash University. Gain deep skills in software development, data, and product thinking.
EntrepreneurshipBuild something real — a product, a platform, a creative business. I want to solve real problems with technology and be in the room where it starts, not just as a developer but as a founder.
Creative TechnologyExplore the space where technology meets art, music, and communication. The next generation of creative work will be built by people who understand both sides.

My work

Projects &
Milestones

Real work, honest reflection, and a record of where I've been — and where I'm going.

01
Ongoing
Original Rap & Songwriting

Objective: To develop a distinct creative voice by writing, recording, and performing original rap music that reflects my experiences growing up between Bangladesh and India.

Skills Developed
Advanced creative writing, lyrical construction, storytelling, audio recording, self-direction, and performance confidence.
Achievements
Written multiple original compositions. Performed publicly in school and community settings. Developed a consistent creative practice.
Reflection
Creativity is a discipline, not a mood. Showing up and writing even when it's difficult uses the same muscle as any professional project.
Future Growth
Goal: release an independent EP. Explore music technology and creative entrepreneurship — building tools for other artists.
SongwritingRecordingPerformanceStorytellingCreative Direction
02
Completed
School Leadership & MUN Coordination

Objective: To develop leadership, diplomatic thinking, and public communication skills through formal school roles and the organisation of Model UN events.

Roles Held
MUN Event Coordinator (Cl. 11), Prefect (Cl. 9), House Captain (Cl. 5), President of Literature & Recreation Club.
Achievements
Successfully coordinated MUN events — delegate preparation, logistics, chairing sessions, managing competing priorities under pressure.
Reflection
Organising MUN — not just participating — showed me that the best leaders are often doing the invisible logistical work no one else sees.
Skills Developed
Event management, diplomatic communication, public speaking, persuasive writing, conflict resolution, multi-stakeholder coordination.
Event ManagementDiplomacyPublic SpeakingDebateLeadership
03
In Progress
Website Development & Digital Creativity

Objective: To build practical digital skills by creating websites and experimenting with design — applying what I learn in my IT Diploma to real, working projects.

What I've Built
Websites from scratch using HTML and CSS. Designed content with Canva. Currently deepening Python skills through coursework and self-study.
Skills Developed
HTML, CSS, Python basics, Canva, visual layout thinking, content creation, and translating ideas into functional digital experiences.
Reflection
Building something and seeing it work in a browser is different from writing a song or winning a debate. It taught me patience and how to problem-solve through error messages.
Future Growth
Build a proper personal website. Explore JavaScript and backend development. Eventually create a tool that combines my creative interests with technical skill.

My Personality · 16Personalities.com

ENFJ
The Protagonist
The natural-born leader who builds people up, speaks clearly, and moves toward something meaningful.
Type
Protagonist
ENFJ-T

The result — and what it actually means for me.

My 16Personalities result is ENFJ-T — the Protagonist. When I first read it, I didn't feel like I was reading a personality type. I felt like I was reading a description of why I've made the choices I've made.

ENFJs lead through connection, not authority. They're energised by people, motivated by meaning, and driven to bring others forward with them. That's been true since Class 5, when I became House Captain — not because I wanted a title, but because I genuinely wanted the house to do well. The same instinct made me coordinate MUN events in Class 11 rather than just participate, and made me President of the Literature Club rather than a quiet member.

The Turbulent side (ENFJ-T) is also real. I set high standards for myself. I overthink. I care too much about what others think sometimes. But that same sensitivity makes me a more careful communicator, a more thoughtful leader, and a better writer. I'm learning to work with it rather than against it.

Energy: Extraverted60%
Mind: Intuitive95%
Nature: Feeling76%
Tactics: Judging64%
Identity: Turbulent86%

Honest reflection

Strengths & areas to grow.

✅ My Strengths
Building Genuine Connections
Whether it's a debate partner, a MUN delegate, or a student I tutor — I build trust quickly because I actually listen. This has made me a more effective leader than technical skill alone ever could.
Communicating Vision
As Club President, I had to inspire people who didn't have to listen to me. Writing, speaking, and performing have all sharpened my ability to frame ideas in ways that move people. This is the skill I'm proudest of.
Energising Others
As MUN Coordinator, keeping delegates focused across long sessions was one of the hardest and most satisfying things I've done. I'm the person who re-motivates a stalled team.
Working Toward Meaning
I don't need external pressure to put in effort. If I care about something, I go all in — which is why I've written songs no one asked for, and why I'm already planning companies before finishing my diploma.
⚠️ Growth Areas
Sensitivity to Criticism
I take feedback personally, even when it's meant constructively. Writing rap and putting work out publicly has helped — it teaches you to separate feedback about the work from feedback about yourself.
Avoiding Conflict
My instinct is to smooth things over rather than address difficult conversations directly. The MUN coordination role pushed me to navigate disagreements more directly than felt comfortable at first.
Overextending Myself
If I'm interested in something, I say yes. House Captain, Club President, MUN Coordinator, tutoring, music, web projects, IT studies — the list gets long. Learning to prioritise is an ongoing effort.
Impatience for Results
I want to see impact quickly. Technical learning requires patience with slow progress. Rap has helped here: good lyrics take time, and there's no shortcut.

How this shapes my path

An ENFJ in technology isn't a contradiction — it might be an advantage.

The tech industry often focuses on technical skill as the primary credential. But the most impactful roles — product management, startup founding, creative direction, team leadership — require exactly what ENFJs do well: listening, synthesising, communicating, and bringing people together around a shared goal. My personality type doesn't push me away from technology. It tells me how I'll operate within it. I'll be the person who can translate between the technical team and the people they're building for — who can pitch an idea, lead a project, and keep a team motivated when things get hard. Famous ENFJ leaders include Barack Obama, Oprah Winfrey, and Malala Yousafzai — people who used communication and conviction as tools for genuine impact.

Reference:
NERIS Analytics Limited. (2025). ENFJ Personality (Protagonist). 16Personalities. Retrieved 24 June 2026, from https://www.16personalities.com
